name: HRC_ALERTS_B
description: Contains event and resource alert definition data.

columns.Name: ALERT_ID
columns.Comments: System generated primary key column.

columns.Name: ALERT_TYPE
columns.Comments: Determines the Alert Type. The valid values are based on the look type HRC_ALERT_TYPE.

columns.Name: FILTER_TYPE
columns.Comments: Determine the type of the filter expression

columns.Name: RESOURCE_APPLICATION
columns.Comments: The resource application (e.g. hcmCoreSetupApi).

columns.Name: RESOURCE_PATH
columns.Comments: The resource path (e.g. /resources/latest/employees)

columns.Name: RUN_TYPE
columns.Comments: Determines what the alert repeat frequency. For an alert which is for a Document Resource only On demand is available. The valid values are based on the look type HRC_ALERT_RUN_TYPE. The default value is On demand.

columns.Name: RUN_EXPRESSION
columns.Comments: Specifies the run expression for either a scheduled expression complying to rfc2445. For example: FREQ=YEARLY;INTERVAL=1;BYMONTH=5;BYDAY=2MO; or a feed name.

columns.Name: SCHEDULED_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: Specifies the ESS Request ID for a schedule. If a schedule recurrence has been defined, this is the Absolute Parent ID request (i.e. the top level ESS request).

columns.Name: SCHEDULED_START
columns.Comments: Specifies the ESS start for a schedule.

columns.Name: SCHEDULED_END
columns.Comments: Specifies the ESS end for a schedule.

columns.Name: KEEP
columns.Comments:
Determines if the alert should be kept active and based on YES_NO.
If Y, the KEEP_FREQ_TYPE, KEEP_VALUE must be specified. If N, the KEEP_FREQ_TYPE, KEEP_VALUE should be null. Note: If the KEEP is updated from Y to N then any existing Keeps are deleted.

columns.Name: KEEP_FREQ_TYPE
columns.Comments: Determines the alert keep frequency type. The valid values are based on the look type HRC_ALERT_KEEP_FREQ_TYPE. The value is mandatory if the KEEP is defined as Y.

columns.Name: KEEP_VALUE
columns.Comments: Determines the alert keep value. The value is mandatory is the KEEP is defined as Y.

columns.Name: THRESHOLD
columns.Comments: Determines the maximum number of Alerts that can be raised per run. A null value indicates no threshold is applied and the number of alerts raised are unlimited.

columns.Name: TEST_MODE
columns.Comments: Determines if the Alert when run is in a test mode. When in test mode, the alert will process normally but will not physically deliver payloads to consumers. Valid values are based on the lookup type YES_NO and the default value is N.

columns.Name: ENABLED
columns.Comments: Determines if the Alert is enabled or disabled. Valid values are based on the lookup type YES_NO and the default value is Y.

columns.Name: FILTER_EXPRESSIONS
columns.Comments: XML structured document of filter expressions.

columns.Name: OBJECT_GUID
columns.Comments: Speicifies the object GUID. Primarily used for seed data framework.

columns.Name: ALERT_CODE
columns.Comments: Specifies an alert code which can be used to identify an alert

columns.Name: ALERT_ACCESS_LEVEL
columns.Comments: ALERT_ACCESS_LEVEL

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATE_HISTORY
columns.Comments: Specifies if Activity History should be generated. Valid values are Y or N

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: RUN_OPTIONS
columns.Comments: Contains config run option values for the run.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: DELETED_FLAG
columns.Comments: Used to identify for soft delete.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
